key_insight: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep neural network can jointly predict penetration state, penetration depth, and weld seam morphology from in-process weld pool images and welding parameters.
engineering_relevance: This enables real-time in-situ quality control for laser welding, reducing reliance on post-process inspection while improving process stability and defect avoidance.
method: CMOS camera captures top weld pool images during welding.
CNN + state-space model extracts spatial and temporal features from images plus welding parameters.
A carefully constructed dataset improves robustness and generalization for multi-task prediction.
evidence_signal: On the test set, the model achieved 99.35% penetration-state accuracy, 1.79 mm penetration-depth error, and 95.65% cross-section reconstruction accuracy.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and coverage of the training dataset, so generalization to new materials, geometries, or welding regimes should be verified before deployment.
paper_title: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep neural network for predicting penetration depth and morphology in laser welding
